question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an integer overflow by subscribing with a limit value close to Long.MAX_VALUE so that the cast `(int) (filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get())` wraps to a large negative integer, causing `Math.min(limit, context.getMaxPageSize())` to return a negative pageSize that is passed to `topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter)`, leading to undefined repository behavior or a full-table scan?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a subscription with no limit set so that `limit` is assigned `Integer.MAX_VALUE` and `pageSize = Math.min(Integer.MAX_VALUE, context.getMaxPageSize())` causes the repository to load up to 5000 rows per poll into a `List` in memory on every 20ms unthrottled cycle, exhausting JVM heap across many concurrent subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user open an unlimited number of concurrent gRPC `subscribeTopic` streams, each creating a new `PollingContext` with `numRepeats = Long.MAX_VALUE` when throttled, causing the shared `Schedulers.boundedElastic()` thread pool to be exhausted and denying service to all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an infinite retry storm by subscribing to a topic while the database is degraded, since `Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1))` retries indefinitely, causing the `boundedElastic()` scheduler to be saturated with retry tasks and amplifying database load until the service crashes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: PollingContext()] Can an unprivileged external user force the unthrottled path (throttled=false) to issue database queries every 20ms with a page size of 5000 rows by repeatedly opening subscriptions, causing a database connection and CPU exhaustion denial-of-service against the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a `consensusTimestamp` arithmetic overflow by subscribing to a topic whose last message has `consensusTimestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE`, so that `last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1` wraps to `Long.MIN_VALUE`, causing `startTime` to be set to the minimum possible timestamp and triggering a full historical data scan from the beginning of time?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the `isComplete()` termination check by crafting a subscription where `total.get()` overshoots `filter.getLimit()` due to a race condition between `onNext()` incrementing `total` and `isComplete()` checking `filter.getLimit() == total.get()` with exact equality, causing the polling loop to never terminate and run indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the throttled `isComplete()` condition `pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize()` by ensuring each poll returns exactly `maxPageSize` messages, preventing the completion signal from ever being set and keeping the subscription alive indefinitely with `numRepeats = Long.MAX_VALUE`?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between `context.getPageSize().set(0L)` in `poll()` and `pageSize.incrementAndGet()` in `onNext()` running concurrently on the reactive scheduler, causing `pageSize` to be reset mid-emission and making `isComplete()` return a stale false result, leading to extra unnecessary database polls?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user inject malicious content into application logs by controlling the `subscriberId` field (derived from `RandomStringUtils.random`) through a crafted gRPC request that sets a custom `subscriberId`, causing log injection via the `[{}] Finished retrieving {} messages` log statement that includes `filter.getSubscriberId()` unsanitized?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user cause information disclosure by triggering debug-level logging of `log.debug(\

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user hold a gRPC subscription open for the full `timeout` duration (default 60 seconds) with `numRepeats = Long.MAX_VALUE` and `pollingFrequency = 2s`, issuing 30 database queries per subscription, and by opening many such subscriptions simultaneously cause a database connection pool exhaustion denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a `Stream` resource leak by cancelling a gRPC subscription mid-poll, since `Flux.fromStream(topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter))` wraps a JDBC `Stream` that may not be closed when the reactive pipeline is cancelled, leading to unclosed database cursors and eventual connection pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all historical topic messages for any topic by setting `startTime=0` in the gRPC `ConsensusTopicQuery`, since `poll()` uses `filter.getStartTime()` as the initial query start time with no access control, allowing full historical data exfiltration from any topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user cause metric cardinality explosion in the `ObservationRegistry` by opening many subscriptions with different `topicId` values, since `Micrometer.observation(observationRegistry)` tagged with the `METRIC` name creates a new metric series per unique label combination, exhausting Micrometer registry memory?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: PollingContext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the `Schedulers.boundedElastic()` thread pool to be exhausted by opening many concurrent subscriptions, since each `PollingTopicMessageRetriever` instance creates its own `boundedElastic()` scheduler at construction time and each subscription schedules polling tasks on it, leading to thread starvation for all other reactive operations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a negative `pageSize` to be passed to `topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter)` by having `context.getTotal().get()` exceed `filter.getLimit()` (due to concurrent `onNext` calls), making `(int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get())` negative, and then `Math.min(negative, maxPageSize)` returns the negative value as the limit, causing unexpected repository behavior?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the `retrieverProperties.isEnabled()` check by exploiting a race condition where the enabled flag is read as true at subscription time but the retriever is disabled mid-stream, causing the polling loop to continue running even after the retriever is administratively disabled?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger `onComplete()` to be called twice (once via `doOnCancel` and once via `doOnComplete`) by cancelling a subscription at the exact moment it completes naturally, causing duplicate log entries and potentially double-decrementing any shared counters that depend on completion signals?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a full-table scan on the `topic_message` table by subscribing with `startTime=0` and no limit, since `poll()` builds `newFilter` with `startTime=0` and `limit=Integer.MAX_VALUE`, issuing a query with no effective upper bound that scans all historical records for the given topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service by subscribing to a high-volume topic with no limit and `throttled=true`, keeping `numRepeats = Long.MAX_VALUE` and polling every 2 seconds indefinitely until the 60-second timeout, issuing 30 database queries that each return up to 1000 rows, consuming significant database I/O?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: PollingContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unthrottled path where `isComplete()` only returns true when `limitHit` is true (ignoring page size), meaning a subscription with no limit on the unthrottled path (`throttled=false`) will always poll `maxPolls=12` times regardless of data exhaustion, issuing unnecessary database queries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user amplify database load by rapidly reconnecting after each subscription times out, since the 60-second `timeout` resets on each new subscription and there is no per-client connection rate limiting, allowing a single attacker to maintain continuous database polling pressure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the `newFilter` built via `filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build()` to have a `startTime` that exceeds `endTime` when `last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1` surpasses the original `endTime`, potentially causing the repository query to return no results but the polling loop to continue issuing queries until timeout?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: '] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the `RepeatSpec.create(r -> !context.isComplete(), context.getNumRepeats())` predicate to be evaluated in a race with `onNext()` updating `total` and `pageSize`, leading to a TOCTOU condition where `isComplete()` returns false just before the last message is processed, causing one extra unnecessary database poll?
